blocks.statement: I can write p = m·v, give the units (kg·m/s), compute momentum for a moving object, and explain why momentum is a VECTOR (sign and direction matter).

blocks.whatWeKnow: 2026-XJ's mass is ~10⁹ kg; its speed (with respect to Earth) is ~30 km/s.
blocks.connection: Today we put a NUMBER on what makes the asteroid hard to stop. Spoiler: the number is enormous, and the rest of Unit 3 figures out what to do with that fact.

blocks.markdown:
## Momentum: mass in motion

**p = m·v** — units: **kg·m/s**.

Momentum is a **vector**: it points the same direction as the velocity. That's not bookkeeping pedantry — when two objects move in *opposite* directions, one of them carries **negative** momentum, and the negatives do real work in every calculation this unit.

The rule for change: **Δp = p_f − p_i = m·(v_f − v_i)**, with signed velocities. Pick a positive direction first, assign signs second, compute third.

terms.definition: A measure of how hard it is to stop a moving object. p = m·v. Vector — has the same direction as the velocity. A 1500-kg car at 30 m/s has p = 45,000 kg·m/s; a 0.05-kg bullet at 400 m/s has p = 20 kg·m/s. The car wins.
terms.cognate: Sp. cantidad de movimiento (momento lineal) · Pt. quantidade de movimento · HC kantite mouvman

blocks.title: Momentum is NOT the same as speed
blocks.markdown: A slow heavy thing can have MORE momentum than a fast light thing. A drifting cruise ship out-momentums a rifle bullet by orders of magnitude.

blocks.patternPrompt: Compute p for each object you choose (try: bullet, sprinter, car, loaded truck, cruise ship). Rank biggest to smallest. What surprised you about the spread?

blocks.prompt: A 1500-kg car moves east at 30 m/s. Find its momentum (magnitude AND direction).
blocks.answer: 4.5 × 10⁴ kg·m/s east. (East matters — p is a vector.)

blocks.prompt: 2026-XJ has mass m = 1.0 × 10⁹ kg and approaches at v = 3.0 × 10⁴ m/s toward Earth. Find its momentum.
blocks.givenHint: The asteroid's mass and speed (toward Earth).
blocks.equationHint: p = m·v — keep the powers of ten organized.

blocks.prompt: A 0.15-kg baseball moves east at 40 m/s, meets the bat, and bounces back west at 50 m/s. What is the CHANGE in its momentum, Δp = p_f − p_i? (Hint: pick east = positive, so v_f = −50.)
blocks.givenHint: m = 0.15 kg, v_i = +40 m/s, v_f = −50 m/s. Write the sign convention first.
blocks.equationHint: Δp = m·(v_f − v_i) — with SIGNED velocities.

blocks.title: Sign matters
blocks.markdown: If you ignore the negative on v_f, your Δp will look like 1.5 kg·m/s. The real Δp is **−13.5 kg·m/s** — almost 10× bigger. The direction of Δp is opposite v_i.

blocks.prompt: A 0.15-kg baseball moves east at 40 m/s. After the bat, it goes WEST at 50 m/s. Find Δp — force the sign. Then complete the cruise-ship frame.
blocks.frame: Δp = ___ kg·m/s. Momentum is bigger for the cruise ship than the bullet because, even though the ship is ___, its ___ is overwhelmingly larger.

blocks.title: Read & practice
blocks.markdown: Open the reader and read **8.1** (momentum — inertia in motion; what makes momentum large). Answer the questions beside the reading as you go.
